title: cerastium pentandrum subsp. tetrandrum
intro:
Cerastium pentandrum subsp. tetrandrum, also known as the lesser mouse-ear or four-petaled mouse-ear, is a plant species in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is often confused with the closely related Cerastium pentandrum subsp. pentandrum.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.

The health benefits of Cerastium pentandrum subsp. tetrandrum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as eczema, acne, and bronchitis.

In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its effectiveness in treating various health conditions.
